title: marilaunidium sericeum
side_effects: Marilaunidium sericeum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues in individuals with sensitive stomachs, leading to symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. In rare cases, excessive consumption of the plant's extracts can cause allergic reactions, resulting in skin rashes, itching, and respiratory problems. Additionally, using the plant in high doses or for extended periods may interfere with blood clotting and increase the risk of bleeding, particularly in individuals taking anticoagulant medications. Furthermore, some people may experience headaches, dizziness, and fatigue after using the plant improperly, which can be a sign of an underlying interaction or adverse reaction.
